name: Baby Dodds
abstract: Warren "Baby" Dodds (December 24, 1898 – February 14, 1959) was an American jazz drummer born in New Orleans, Louisiana. He is regarded as one of the best jazz drummers of the pre-big band era.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know Baby Dodds, but I heard so much about him from New Orleans. Yes, I knew of Baby Dodds.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t realize – I didn’t get a chance to meet him. But one time when Pearl [Bailey] – I was married to Pearl at that time – when she went to New York, I put on a record of Baby Dodds, and I was amazed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Mason
about_quotes.evidence: and that in that group uh we had uh uh George Foster uh George Foster uh a New Orleans bass player and uh another boy uh Baby Dodds who played the drum

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: That was Muggsy Spanier, Sidney Bechet, Joe Sullivan, Pops Foster, Baby Dodds. The same group that I brought on my Ivy League tour

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: That was Muggsy Spanier, Sidney Bechet, Joe Sullivan, Pops Foster, Baby Dodds. The same group that I brought on my Ivy League tour

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: It was Jimmy and myself, and Baby Dodds was there. It sounds funny now to say I got to play with him, but I was thrilled.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
